summary: Session 5 testing complete. Backend pytest 18/18 PASS (6 grocery + 6 misc + 6 todos including HEUTE-injection date freshness, stale date null-out, rate limit shared across 3 modes). Frontend: Tasks/Chores tabs render correctly, smart-date parser hints all 4 variants spot-on (morgenâ†’Di 21.04 09:00, in 3 Tagenâ†’Do 23.04 23:59, naechste Wocheâ†’Mo 27.04 09:00, Ende der Wocheâ†’Fr 24.04 23:59), hint click fills datetime field correctly. AddTodo form, AddChore form (all 4 freq chips + custom-days input), WeeklyStats with trophy đźŹ†, BrainDump todos UI all render. CRITICAL BUG: BrainDump todos parse â€” backend returns 200 but UI displays 'Netzwerkfehler. Bitte Verbindung prĂĽfen.' due to ReferenceError on undefined `todosMembers` variable. Persistence flows untestable: todos/chores/chore_completions tables not provisioned in Supabase (404 from REST API) â€” user must run /app/supabase_session5_setup.sql; no service-role key available to provision via admin tooling.

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: `todosMembers` is not defined in BrainDump component (line 105). Only `todosCtx` exists in PreviewRow (line 340). When items contain non-empty assignee_hint, the .find() throws â†’ caught by try/catch â†’ user sees 'Netzwerkfehler. Bitte Verbindung prĂĽfen.' even though backend returned 200 with valid items. Confirmed via network log: `200 POST /api/brain-dump/parse` succeeded, but no preview rows ever render.

integration_issues.issue: Supabase tables `todos`, `chores`, `chore_completions` and RPC `archive_old_todos` not provisioned (404 verified via REST API for all 3 tables). All write operations from frontend will silently fail. EXPECTED â€” user must run /app/supabase_session5_setup.sql in Supabase SQL editor. No admin/service key available in env to provision automatically.

Smart date hints â€” all 4 variants verified at /app/frontend/src/utils/smartDate.js: 'morgen'â†’Di 21.04 09:00, 'in 3 Tagen'â†’Do 23.04 23:59, 'nĂ¤chste Woche'â†’Mo 27.04 09:00, 'Ende der Woche'â†’Fr 24.04 23:59

TodoRow overdue red pulse animation (needs persisted overdue todo)

action_items: [CRITICAL â€” main agent fix] BrainDump.jsx line 105: `todosMembers` is undefined. Add `const todosCtx = useTodos(); const todosMembers = todosCtx?.houseMembers || [];` near top of BrainDump component (around line 30) â€” currently only PreviewRow has it. Without this, todos brain-dump UI is completely unusable: backend returns 200 but UI says 'Netzwerkfehler'.
[USER ACTION] Run /app/supabase_session5_setup.sql in Supabase SQL editor to provision todos, chores, chore_completions tables + archive_old_todos RPC. Required for any persistence test.
[Re-test after fix] After main agent fixes todosMembers and user provisions tables, re-run frontend Playwright for end-to-end todos/chores flows.
critical_code_review_comments: BrainDump.jsx â€” undefined `todosMembers` reference (line 105) is hidden by surrounding try/catch, masking as a network error. Worth adding a console.error in the catch block so future bugs of this type aren't silently labelled as 'Netzwerkfehler'.
BrainDump.jsx grew to 503 lines combining 3 modes (grocery/misc/todos) â€” splitting per-mode preview rows or extracting hooks would reduce risk of similar undefined-variable mistakes.

test_brain_dump_misc.py and test_brain_dump_todos.py do not provide a fallback for REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env (test_brain_dump.py does). Tests fail collection if env var not exported in shell. Recommend matching pattern across all 3 files.

success_rate.frontend: render+UI: 100%; brain-dump-todos integration: 0% (blocked by undefined var); persistence: blocked by missing Supabase tables (env, not bug)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully validated (18/18 pytest including HEUTE injection + stale date null-out + 3-mode rate limit). Frontend smart-date parser perfect for all 4 spec variants. ONE CRITICAL FRONTEND BUG: BrainDump.jsx references undefined `todosMembers` causing brain-dump-todos parse to surface 'Netzwerkfehler' even on 200 OK backend response. Fix: declare `const todosCtx = useTodos(); const todosMembers = todosCtx?.houseMembers || [];` inside the BrainDump component (NOT just in PreviewRow). Persistence flows untestable until user runs /app/supabase_session5_setup.sql â€” no service-role key available to provision programmatically. After SQL run + bug fix, re-test: TodoRow overdue red-pulse, nudge button (assigned to Iris), Completed/Archive collapsibles, ChoreCard 1x/2x_week progress bar fill + âś… flip, completion dots, full brain-dump-todos save flow.
rca_of_issue: BrainDump.jsx has `useTodos` imported (line 8) and used inside the inner `PreviewRow` component (line 340: `const todosCtx = useTodos()`). However, line 105 inside the parent `BrainDump` component's `handleParse` references `todosMembers` which was never declared in this scope. When the AI returns todo items with a non-empty `assignee_hint` (always true in practice â€” backend always returns at least 'iris' or 'wir'), `todosMembers.find(...)` throws ReferenceError. The surrounding try/catch (line 119-121) catches ALL exceptions and unconditionally sets the error to 'Netzwerkfehler. Bitte Verbindung prĂĽfen.', misleadingly suggesting a network failure. Reproduction: Login â†’ Tasks â†’ KI Brain Dump (todos) â†’ enter any text mentioning a person â†’ KI-Parse â†’ backend returns 200 but UI shows 'Netzwerkfehler' and clears the form. Mitigation: Move `const todosCtx = useTodos(); const todosMembers = todosCtx?.houseMembers || [];` to the top of the BrainDump component body.
